summary: Ukrainian air defenses shot down 96 hostile drones on 21 June, while Russian missile attacks struck multiple locations across Ukraine including Zaporizhzhia and Crimea. Fires were reported at the Tavriya power plant, a gas distribution station in Simferopol, and the Kerch sea port following Ukrainian strikes. Russian forces also launched Shahed, Gerbera, and Kinzhal attacks on several oblasts, causing casualties and infrastructure damage.
key_points: Ukrainian forces downed 96 enemy Shahed, Gerbera, Italmash and other drones across the north, south, east and centre on 21 June (Source: @kpszsu/65808).
Russian forces fired two Iskander‑M/S‑400 ballistic missiles from Voronezh and two Kinzhal aero‑ballistic missiles from Ryazan, hitting six locations (Source: @kpszsu/65808).
Ukrainian strikes ignited fires at Tavriya thermal power plant, a gas distribution station in Simferopol and an oil‑gas depot of TESCO in Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/82067).
A Russian drone attack on the Kerch sea port caused fires at the port, the Caucasus port area and military unit 98546 (Source: @UkraineNow/82086).

Multiple Shahed attacks were reported from Kyiv, Zhytomyr, Chernihiv and Sumy regions, with several incidents of UAVs heading toward Kremenchuk, Cherkasy and Kyiv suburbs (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67936, @mon1tor_ua/67954).
Russian Kinzhal missiles were launched from Chernihiv towards Kyiv region, specifically Vylkove/Kiliya in Odesa (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67971).
Four civilians were killed and 28 injured by a Ukrainian drone strike on the Crimean peninsula, according to Russian reports (Source: @tass_agency/381676).
Russian attacks with KAB missiles on Zaporizhzhia caused casualties, including four dead and six injured (Source: @UkraineNow/82079).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defenses downed 96 hostile drones on 21 June, while Russian missile and drone attacks struck multiple Ukrainian regions, including Zaporizhzhia and Crimea. Ukrainian strikes set fire to key energy facilities in Crimea, notably the Tavriya power plant and a gas distribution station. Russian drone attacks on the Kerch sea port caused additional fires and civilian casualties.

points: Ukrainian forces reported shooting down 96 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmash and other UAVs across the country on the night of 21 June, covering the north, south, east and centre (Source: @kpszsu/65808).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted a Shahed UAV heading toward Vylkove/Kiliya in Odesa, part of a series of attacks from the Black Sea (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67961).
Ukrainian artillery struck the Tavriya thermal power plant, a gas distribution station in Simferopol and an oil‑gas depot of TESCO in Crimea, causing multiple fires (Source: @UkraineNow/82067).
Ukrainian forces launched a high‑speed target from Sumy towards Chernihiv (Source: @kpszsu/65772).
Ukrainian drones attacked the Kerch sea port, igniting fires at the port, the Caucasus port area and military unit 98546 (Source: @UkraineNow/82086).
Ukrainian Kinzhal‑type attacks were reported from Chernihiv towards Kyiv region, specifically Vylkove/Kiliya (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67971).
Ukrainian forces reported a missile passing through Kyiv oblast toward Zhytomyr (Source: @kpszsu/65770).
Ukrainian air defense units engaged a fast‑moving target heading to Kremenchuk from an unspecified direction (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67936).
Russian forces launched two Iskander‑M/S‑400 ballistic missiles from Voronezh region on 21 June, striking Ukrainian targets (Source: @kpszsu/65808).
Two Kinzhal aero‑ballistic missiles were fired from Ryazan airspace, hitting Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/65808).
Shahed, Gerbera and Italmash drones were launched from multiple Russian directions including Oryol, Kursk, Bryansk, Millerovo, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, Kacha and Chauda (Source: @kpszsu/65808).
Russian drone attacks on the Crimean peninsula resulted in four civilian deaths and 28 injuries (Source: @tass_agency/381676).
Russian KAB missile strikes on Zaporizhzhia caused four fatalities and six injuries, damaging private houses (Source: @UkraineNow/82079).
Russian forces reported a temporary power outage in part of Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @tass_agency/381639).
Russian authorities warned of potential ballistic weapon use across Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/65750).

Russian Shahed attacks were reported from the direction of Oryol and Kursk affecting Sumy oblast (Source: @kpszsu/65808).

Russian Kinzhal missiles were launched from the Ryazan region, impacting eastern Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/65808).
Ukrainian reports of UAVs moving southward along the Sumy‑Chernihiv border (Source: @kpszsu/65809).
Russian artillery strike “Sunchpeck” reportedly destroyed Ukrainian positions in Sumy region (Source: @tass_agency/381661).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down Shahed drones over northern Kyiv region heading west (Source: @kpszsu/65809).
Russian UAVs were observed over northern Kyiv heading westward (Source: @kpszsu/65809).
Ukrainian reports of a UAV over the north of Sumy heading toward Królevets and Konotop (Source: @kpszsu/65805).

Ukrainian forces reported a fast‑moving target heading toward Kremenchuk from the north (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67936).

Ukrainian air defense engaged a high‑speed target over the north of Kyiv oblast (Source: @kpszsu/65777).
Ukrainian reports of UAVs moving southward along the border with Belarus near Slaвутич (Source: @kpszsu/65800).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defenses shot down 96 hostile drones across multiple regions on 21 June, indicating high‑intensity UAV activity (Source: @kpszsu/65808).
Russian forces launched two Iskander‑M/S‑400 ballistic missiles and two Kinzhal aero‑ballistic missiles, striking six Ukrainian locations (Source: @kpszsu/65808).
Ukrainian strikes ignited fires at the Tavriya power plant, a gas distribution station in Simferopol and an oil‑gas depot in Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/82067).
Russian drone attacks on the Kerch sea port caused fires at the port, the Caucasus port area and military unit 98546 (Source: @UkraineNow/82086).
Four civilians were killed and 28 injured by a Ukrainian‑claimed drone strike on the Crimean peninsula, according to Russian reports (Source: @tass_agency/381676).
Russian KAB missile strikes on Zaporizhzhia resulted in four deaths, six injuries and damage to at least 20 private houses (Source: @UkraineNow/82079).
Multiple Shahed UAVs were launched from Russian regions (Oryol, Kursk, Bryansk, Ryazan) and intercepted by Ukrainian defenses (Source: @kpszsu/65808).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian missile and drone launches, including Iskander‑M, Kinzhal and Shahed UAVs, demonstrate a sustained long‑range strike capability targeting critical infrastructure across Ukraine. Ukrainian air defenses have shown effectiveness, downing a large number of UAVs, but the continued use of ballistic missiles poses a high‑risk threat to energy and civilian sites. The firestorms in Crimea and Zaporizhzhia highlight the vulnerability of energy assets to aerial attacks, potentially degrading regional power supplies.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have mobilized air defense units and mobile fire‑fighting groups to counter the surge in UAV and missile attacks, emphasizing rapid response to protect energy facilities. Russian statements about temporary bridge closures and power outages suggest logistical disruptions aimed at limiting Ukrainian counter‑operations. Internationally, reports of civilian casualties in Crimea may influence diplomatic narratives, while Ukraine's claims of successful strikes on Russian‑occupied territories aim to sustain morale and demonstrate offensive capability.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch corridors from Oryol, Kursk, Bryansk and Ryazan remains a priority to anticipate future Shahed swarms. The pattern of missile launches from Voronezh and Ryazan indicates a coordinated strike timetable that could be forecasted for early warning. Fires at energy sites in Crimea and Zaporizhzhia require close observation for secondary effects on regional power grids. Continued reports of Kinzhal usage suggest Russian testing of hypersonic capabilities against Ukrainian air defenses.
